summary: Task 8 ships a UI-only layer (ViewModel + Compose screen) over the existing ModelRegistry. Full OWASP Top 10 sweep shows no critical or high-severity vulnerabilities: no hardcoded secrets, no network/FS/deserialisation code, no user-controlled input reaching sinks, no privilege boundary, no permissions touched, buttons are closure-bound to specific ModelEntry instances so the user cannot target arbitrary model names. The new dependency androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-runtime-compose:2.8.7 is an official AndroidX artefact from Google Maven, pinned via the shared lifecycle version ref — no supply-chain concern. Two low-severity observations: (1) raw errorMessage produced by the download worker is rendered verbatim in the FAILED card and may leak internal file paths / stack traces to the UI, and (2) MutableSharedFlow(extraBufferCapacity = 1, replay = 0) buffers an OpenChat event emitted while the screen is out of composition, which can fire an unexpected navigation on re-entry — more a UX/robustness note than a security issue.

findings.issue: downloadStatus.errorMessage is rendered verbatim to the user in the FAILED branch via stringResource(R.string.model_error_prefix, ...). The message originates from WorkManager outputData (DownloadRepository.kt:190) and can contain internal file paths, HTTP URLs with query strings, or exception stack-trace fragments depending on how the worker reports failures. This is an information-disclosure / log-exposure concern (OWASP A09, CWE-209 — Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information). Compose Text rendering is plain-text, so there is no XSS vector; the concern is leakage to the user and potentially to screenshots / accessibility services.
findings.suggestion: Sanitise the surface form before display: either (a) map known exception classes to a short localised message in the worker and only pass a safe token (e.g. 'network_timeout', 'disk_full') through errorMessage, or (b) keep the raw message in logs only and display a generic stringResource(R.string.model_status_failed) in the UI, using errorMessage strictly for Logcat. At minimum, strip absolute file paths and URL query strings before putting the text into ModelDownloadStatus.errorMessage.

findings.issue: MutableSharedFlow<NavEvent>(extraBufferCapacity = 1) with implicit replay = 0 defends against rotation replay (good, matches the spec's stated reason for choosing SharedFlow over StateFlow). However, extraBufferCapacity = 1 means an OpenChat emit fired while no collector is subscribed (e.g. screen briefly out of composition) is buffered and delivered to the next subscriber on re-entry, which can trigger an unintended navigation. Not an auth/authz bypass — the user still initiated the original click — but worth hardening to avoid surprise side-effects, especially once Task 10 wires a real NavHost.
findings.suggestion: Consider using extraBufferCapacity = 0 with onBufferOverflow = BufferOverflow.DROP_OLDEST, or Channel<NavEvent>(Channel.BUFFERED).receiveAsFlow() which has truly one-shot semantics. Alternatively, document in the ViewModel KDoc that buffered events are intentional so the Task 10 reviewer knows what to expect.
